data.content:
# Exercise
## Starting point
- Channel **funded**
- Bob already holds **commitment #1**
- Channel uses a **CSV timeout** (e.g., 144 blocks)

## Task 1 — Second Payment (25,000 sats)
- Bob requests +25,000 sats
- Alice builds **commitment #2** (use a ~1,000 sat fee)
- Alice sends **preimage + signature** to Bob

## Task 2 — Deep Verification
For commitment #2, Bob must:
- Perform all standard checks (inputs, outputs, TXID, scriptPubKey)  
- **New**: run **full script verification** for the spending path

## Task 3 — Channel Closure
- Bob decides to **close**: add Bob's signature to commitment #2 and **broadcast**
- Confirm on‑chain that Bob's output equals **(#1 + #2)** (minus any fees)

## Task 4 — Timeout Refund (New Channel)
**Setup new channel from scratch**:
- Generate **fresh keys** for Alice and Bob
- Create new **2-of-2 multisig** redeem script
- Alice gets Bob's signature on **refund tx** (144 block CSV)
- Alice broadcasts **new funding tx** (135,000 sats)

**Scenario**: Bob disappears immediately after channel setup (never provides any commitments).

Alice must:
- Wait for **144 blocks** after new funding tx confirmation
- Add Alice's signature to the **refund transaction**
- **Broadcast refund tx** with scriptSig:
  ```
  <OP_0> <Alice_sig> <Bob_sig> <redeemScript>
  ```
- Verify Alice recovers **full channel balance** minus fees

**Key insight**: The refund mechanism protects Alice even if Bob disappears immediately after funding, before any payments are made.
data.title: Text Info Node
Check Result
data.comment: node which sets global error if at least one of the inputs is not true

data.content:
# Spilman Channels — Bitcoin’s First Payment Channel

An intro to **unidirectional** payment channels: pay many times off‑chain, touch the blockchain twice (open/close), and stay safe with a pre‑signed refund.

## Why this exists
Blocks are scarce and slow. Channels batch thousands of updates off‑chain:
- **Throughput**: many updates, two on‑chain txs
- **Finality for users**: instant receipts off‑chain; eventual settlement on‑chain

## Sequence numbers, fixed
Satoshi's original `nSequence` idea for replacement didn't work—miners could always choose any valid version based on fees, not sequence numbers. In 2016:
- **BIP‑68**: repurposes `nSequence` as a **relative timelock** (CSV)
- **BIP‑112**: script‑level CSV (`OP_CHECKSEQUENCEVERIFY`)
Spilman channels rely on a **CSV‑delayed refund** so Alice can reclaim funds if Bob disappears.

## How a Spilman channel works (high level)
1) **Set up** a 2‑of‑2 multisig (Alice + Bob)  

2) **Safety first**: Bob signs a **refund tx** spending the funding output, with `nSequence = delay`  

3) **Fund**: Alice broadcasts the funding tx to the multisig  

4) **Pay**: for each payment, Alice creates a **new commitment tx** paying Bob more, signs it, and sends it to Bob  

5) **Close**: Bob broadcasts the **latest** commitment; or Alice uses the **refund** after the delay

## What you’ll build in this lesson
- Generate keys, build the 2‑of‑2 **redeem script**
- Construct & validate the **refund** (CSV) before funding
- Fund the channel and verify on‑chain
- Produce **commitment transactions** for payments and verify them
- Close cooperatively (Bob broadcasts latest commit) or via timeout (Alice’s refund)

## Limits that point to Lightning
- **Unidirectional** (Alice → Bob only)  
- **No routing** (only direct peers)  
- **Refund path has fixed delay** (if Bob disappears, Alice waits the CSV timeout before reclaiming funds)
- **Pre‑SegWit malleability risk** (funding TXID changes break pre‑sigs)

*Learning Spilman cements the core ideas behind modern bidirectional channels and HTLCs.*
data.title: Text Info Node

data.content:
# Setup: Keys & Channel Parameters (Alice ⇄ Bob)

- **Fresh keypairs**: generate dedicated keys for this channel only
- **Refund delay**: agree on the CSV delay (e.g., **144 blocks**)
- **Channel capacity**: agree on total sats to lock
- *(Optional)* fee policy: confirm target **sat/vB** and network (testnet/regtest)
data.title: Text Info Node

data.content:
# Draft the Funding Transaction (do **not** broadcast yet)

- **Input**: Alice’s confirmed UTXO  
- **Output**: P2SH **2‑of‑2 multisig** (Alice + Bob)  
- **Fees & policy**: choose a sane fee rate; set `nSequence` for RBF if desired  
- **Safety rule**: funding is broadcast **only after Bob’s refund signature is verified**
data.title: Text Info Node

data.content:
# Build the Refund Transaction → Send to Bob

- **Input**: `funding_txid:0` (the multisig output)  
- **CSV delay**: set `nSequence = agreed_blocks` (e.g., **144**); enforces *relative* lock  
- **Output**: back to **Alice’s P2PKH** (capacity − fee)    
- **Share with Bob**: provide the refund tx (preimage details) so Bob can verify & sign
data.title: Text Info Node

data.content:
# Bob Verifies the Refund → Signs

- **Extract redeem script** from the refund’s script template  
- **Check P2SH binding**: `HASH160(redeem_script)` equals the funding **scriptHash**  
- **Check CSV**: `nSequence ==` agreed **block delay**  
- **Sign** with **SIGHASH_ALL** and return the signature to Alice
data.title: Text Info Node

data.content:
# Alice Verifies Bob’s Refund Signature

- Verify **Bob’s signature** over the refund preimage (SIGHASH_ALL)  
- If valid, it’s now **safe to broadcast** the funding transaction
data.title: Text Info Node

data.content:
# Bob Confirms Funding On‑Chain

- See the funding tx in **mempool / block**  
- Verify **amount** equals negotiated channel capacity  
- Verify **scriptPubKey** matches the expected **P2SH** (multisig hash)  
- Wait for **≥1 confirmation** (or your chosen threshold)  
- Record the **TXID** you will reference in commitments
data.title: Text Info Node

data.content:
# Bob Requests Payment → Alice Creates Commitment Tx

- **Bob** specifies: *“N sats for X”*  
- **Alice** drafts a **commitment transaction** spending the funding output:  
  - Output to **Bob** for the requested amount  
  - Output to **Alice** for change (minus fee)  
- **Sign** with **SIGHASH_ALL**  
- **Send** the commitment **preimage + Alice’s signature** to Bob
data.title: Text Info Node

data.content:
# Bob Verifies the Commitment → Stage for Broadcast

- Recompute the **hash** of the commitment preimage; verify **Alice’s signature**  
- Confirm **input TXID** equals the **confirmed funding** TXID  
- Check **Bob’s output amount**, **total outputs**, and **scriptPubKey** semantics  
- Validate the **previous scriptPubKey** reference matches the funding redeem/script hash  
- **Store the latest commitment** (tx + Alice’s sig); deliver the service
data.title: Text Info Node

data.content:
# Deliver the Goods/Services

With the latest **valid commitment** in hand (tx + Alice’s signature):
- Provide the agreed goods/services
- Keep the **latest** commitment ready; **only the newest** should be broadcast
data.title: Text Info Node

data.content:
# Lesson 6: Spilman (Unidirectional) Payment Channel

- Create a 2‑of‑2 multisig redeem script and its P2SH address for the channel (Alice + Bob).
- Prepare a refund transaction from the yet‑to‑be‑funded multisig back to Alice, using a relative timelock (`nSequence`/CSV) as a safety timeout.
- Have Bob verify and co‑sign the refund before any funds are committed.
- Fund the multisig on‑chain after the refund is secured.
- Make off‑chain payments by updating a “latest commitment” transaction that splits funds; Alice signs and sends it to Bob.
- Bob can complete the signature and broadcast the most recent commitment at any time before the timeout to settle on‑chain.
data.title: Text Info Node
